extracted_knowledge.title: AI-based eye tracking technology for screening ADHD symptoms in children
extracted_knowledge.abstract: OBJECTIVE: To explore the potential of using artificial intelligence (AI)-based eye tracking technology on a tablet for screening Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) symptoms in children...
key_findings: CNN-based eye tracking on tablet reliably discriminated ADHD (n=112) from TD (n=325) children
Diagnosis and age had significant effects on fixation task performance
Tablet-based screening provides potential for ADHD assessment outside clinical settings
extracted_knowledge.methods_detail: End-to-end CNN appearance-based model; prosaccade/antisaccade paradigm (14+14 trials); two-way MANOVA
limitations: Binary classification only (ADHD vs TD), no subtype differentiation

knowledge_gaps: No subtype differentiation — binary ADHD vs TD only
